content.Text:
[Tue 2026-04-21 15:18 PDT] Create a new interactive tool: Graph Influence Maximizer (docs/influence-maximizer.html).

This is for the GraphVisual project - a Java graph visualization app with many interactive HTML tools in docs/.

## What to Build

An interactive HTML page for **influence spread simulation** on graphs. The user builds/loads a graph, picks seed nodes, and watches influence cascade through the network. The tool autonomously recommends optimal seed sets.

## Technical Requirements

- Single self-contained HTML file at `docs/influence-maximizer.html`
- Uses `<link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css">` for theming (dark theme with CSS variables: --bg, --surface, --border, --text, --text-muted, --accent, --accent-hover, --surface-hover)
- Canvas-based graph visualization with draggable nodes
- NO external dependencies (no CDN imports)

## Features

### Core
1. **Graph Input**: 8 presets (Karate Club, Small World, Scale-Free, Star, Grid, Ring, Random, Complete) + text editor for custom adjacency lists
2. **Influence Models**: Independent Cascade (IC) and Linear Threshold (LT) with configurable propagation probability (0.01-1.0) and threshold
3. **Seed Selection**: Click nodes to toggle as seeds (highlighted in accent color)
4. **Spread Simulation**: Animated step-by-step cascade visualization showing newly activated nodes per step
5. **Auto-run button**: Run 100 Monte Carlo simulations to estimate expected spread for current seed set

### Autonomous/Agentic Features
6. **Greedy Seed Optimizer**: Autonomous greedy algorithm that finds the k best seed nodes (k configurable 1-10) by marginal gain. Shows progress bar during computation.
7. **Influence Heatmap**: Color nodes by their influence potential (how much spread they'd cause if seeded alone)
8. **Campaign Planner**: Given a target coverage % (e.g., 80%), autonomously determines minimum seed set needed, with budget estimation
9. **Proactive Insights Panel**: Auto-generated recommendations like:
   - "Node X is a super-spreader — seeding it alone reaches 45% of network"
   - "Nodes A and B have overlapping influence — pick one, not both"
   - "Current seed set has diminishing returns — 3 seeds reach 70%, but 4th only adds 3%"
   - "Network has isolated clusters — need seeds in each for full coverage"

### UI Layout
- Left sidebar (300px): Controls panel with graph preset selector, model selector, probability slider, seed count, buttons (Simulate, Optimize, Heatmap, Plan Campaign)
- Main area: Canvas graph visualization (top), stats bar (nodes reached, % coverage, steps taken, spread efficiency), insights panel (bottom)
- Stats bar with cards showing: Seeds, Reached, Coverage %, Steps, Efficiency (reached/seeds ratio)

### Visualization
- Inactive nodes: gray
- Seed nodes: accent color (bright)  
- Newly activated (current step): pulsing green
- Previously activated: lighter green
- Edges: dim by default, highlight edges that transmitted influence
- Animation: step-through with play/pause/speed controls
- Heatmap mode: gradient from cool (low influence) to hot (high influence)

### Navigation
- Back link to index.html at top

## Style Patterns
Follow the exact CSS patterns from other tools in the repo. Use classes like: .btn, .btn-primary, .btn-secondary, .control-group, .control-row, .stat-card, .insights-panel, .insight, .tag, etc. Grid layout with sidebar + main area.

## Also Update
Add a link to the new tool in `docs/index.html`. Look at how other tools are linked there and follow the same pattern. Add it in the tools/analysis section with an appropriate icon/emoji.

## Build Verification
After creating the files, verify the HTML is well-formed by checking for matching tags and complete structure.

Creating `docs/influence-maximizer.html` and wiring it into `docs/index.html`. I’m checking the existing `docs/` tools and shared CSS/markup patterns first so the new page matches the project’s conventions.

`rg` is not installed here, so I’m falling back to targeted file reads. Next I’m pulling structure from a few similar tools and the index page so I can mirror the existing layout and card/link patterns exactly.
I’ve got the baseline structure. Now I’m sampling one or two richer analysis tools to reuse the same control-group, stats-bar, and insights-panel conventions rather than inventing new markup.

ToolUse.name: Run Select-String -Path docs/*.html -Pattern "insights-panel|stat-card|control-group|tag|progress" | Select-Object -First 120 | ForEach-Object { $_.Path + ':' + $_.LineNumber + ':' + $_.Line.Trim() }
ToolUse.raw_input: {"call_id":"call_1xIwslO4pzvnvAd45x7ziLDt","turn_id":"019db21f-fc04-7ff1-a9c0-164d53a3bb4a","command":["C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe","-Command","Select-String -Path docs/*.html -Pattern \"insights-panel|stat-card|control-group|tag|progress\" | Select-Object -First 120 | ForEach-Object { $_.Path + ':' + $_.LineNumber + ':' + $_.Line.Trim() }"],"cwd":"C:\\Users\\onlin\\.openclaw\\workspace\\temp-builder","parsed_cmd":[{"type":"unknown","cmd":"Select-String -Path docs/*.html -Pattern \"insights-panel|stat-card|control-group|tag|progress\" | Select-Object -First 120 | ForEach-Object { $_.Path + ':' + $_.LineNumber + ':' + $_.Line.Trim() }"}],"source":"agent"}

GraphVisual

Documentation
Getting Started
🏠Overview 🚀Setup Guide
Tools
🔍Graph Explorer 🧭Path Finder 🔀Diff Visualizer 🔎Query Playground 🎲Graph Generator 📈Statistics 🌈Spectrum 🔢Adjacency Matrix 🎨Graph Coloring 🧩Coloring Game 🎯Centrality Radar ✏️Graph Editor 🔍Community Detection 🌲MST Visualizer 🌊Network Flow 🚶Random Walk 🦠Diffusion Sim 🔄Isomorphism ⚖️Bipartite Checker 🌉Bridges & APs 📐Planarity 🔀Crossing Game 🚨Anomaly Detector 👑Dominating Set 🛡️Vertex Cover 💍Max Matching 🧠Graph Advisor 🧬Evolution Sim 🔮Link Predictor

💻GitHub 📦Releases

📊 GraphVisual Community evolution visualization for student social networks

🚀 Get Started 📖 API Reference 🍳Cookbook ⭐ GitHub

What is GraphVisual?

GraphVisual is a Java desktop application for studying community evolution in student populations using Bluetooth proximity data. It processes meeting records from a PostgreSQL database, classifies social relationships, and renders interactive graph visualizations with timeline playback.

Built for research on social network analysis — specifically understanding how communities form, dissolve, and evolve over time in university settings.

Features

🎨

Interactive Visualization

Drag, zoom, and rotate graph nodes using JUNG's graph mouse. Explore community structures with cluster-based layouts.

⏱️

Timeline Playback

Animate community graphs across 92 days with play/pause/stop controls. Watch relationships form and dissolve.

🏷️

5 Relationship Types

Friends, classmates, study groups, familiar strangers, and strangers — each color-coded on the graph.

🎛️

Adjustable Thresholds

Tune meeting duration and frequency thresholds per relationship type in real-time.

📊

Network Statistics

Real-time metrics: node/edge counts, density, degree stats, hub identification, centrality analysis.

🔬

Community Detection

Connected-component analysis with modularity scoring, community density, and dominant edge-type breakdowns.

🧭

Shortest Path Finder

BFS (hop-count) and Dijkstra (weight-optimal) path finding between any two nodes.

💾

GraphML Export

Export graphs to standard GraphML format for Gephi, Cytoscape, NetworkX, and other tools.

🦠

Diffusion Simulator

Simulate epidemic and information spreading on networks with SIR, SIS, and Independent Cascade models. Proactive containment recommendations.

Relationship Classification

TypeColorLocationDurationFrequency
FriendsGreenPublic areas> 10 min≥ 2/day
ClassmatesBlueClassrooms> 30 min≥ 1/day
Study GroupsOrangeClassrooms> 20 min≤ 1/day
Familiar StrangersGrayPublic/paths< 2 min> 1/day
StrangersRedPublic/paths< 2 min< 2/day

All thresholds are adjustable at runtime via the Category Panel sliders.

Quick Start

# Clone
git clone https://github.com/sauravbhattacharya001/GraphVisual.git
cd GraphVisual

# Configure database
export DB_HOST=localhost DB_USER=your_user DB_PASS=your_pass

# Build with Maven
mvn initialize -P install-local-deps
mvn package -B

# Run
java -jar target/graphvisual-1.1.0-all.jar
See the full setup guide for detailed instructions including Docker, Ant builds, and data pipeline setup.

Tech Stack

TechnologyPurpose
Java 11+Application language
JUNG 2.0.1Graph data structures and visualization
Java SwingDesktop GUI framework
PostgreSQLMeeting and Bluetooth event storage
MavenBuild and dependency management
JUnit 4Unit testing
GitHub ActionsCI/CD, CodeQL, Pages deployment

Documentation

🚀

Setup Guide

Installation, configuration, building, running the data pipeline, Docker deployment.
🏗️

Architecture

Project structure, data pipeline, algorithm details, component design.
🗄️

Database Schema

PostgreSQL table schemas, query patterns, data pipeline ordering.
📖

API Reference

Detailed class and method documentation for all public APIs.

Built by Saurav Bhattacharya • GitHub • Licensed under MIT

GraphVisual

Documentation
Getting Started
🏠Overview 🚀Setup Guide
Tools
🔍Graph Explorer 🧭Path Finder 🔎Query Playground 🎲Graph Generator 📈Statistics 🔢Adjacency Matrix 🎨Graph Coloring ✏️Graph Editor 🔍Community Detection 🌲MST Visualizer 🌊Network Flow 🚶Random Walk 🔄Isomorphism ⚖️Bipartite Checker 🌉Bridges & APs 📐Planarity 🛡️Resilience 📋Topo Sort 🔗Max Clique 🚨Anomaly Detector 👑Dominating Set
Architecture
🏗️Architecture 🗄️Database Schema
Reference
📖API Reference 🍳Cookbook

👑 Dominating Set Finder Find minimum dominating sets with greedy & exact algorithms

A dominating set S of a graph G is a subset of vertices such that every vertex is either in S or adjacent to at least one vertex in S. Finding a minimum dominating set is NP-hard, but greedy approximation works well in practice. This tool lets you visualize both approaches step by step.

0
Nodes
0
Edges
—
Dom. Set Size
—
γ(G) / n
0
Steps
In dominating set
Dominated (covered)
Uncovered
Current candidate

Presets

Algorithm

Custom Input

Step History

No steps yet — run an algorithm

About Dominating Sets

📡

Wireless Networks

Dominating sets model optimal placement of relay nodes — every device is within one hop of a relay.

🏥

Facility Location

Place facilities (hospitals, fire stations) so every community is adjacent to at least one.

🔒

Network Monitoring

Position sensors to observe all network links — a dominating set monitors every neighbor.

🧬

Biological Networks

Identify essential proteins in PPI networks: a dominating set covers all interaction partners.

Built by Saurav Bhattacharya • GitHub • Licensed under MIT
